preamble: The voiced palatal approximant /j/ can undergo a phonetic transformation to the palatal nasal /ɲ/. This shift often occurs through processes of nasalization or glide strengthening, particularly in environments influenced by nasality or across morphological boundaries.
phoneticEffects: Nasalization, Fortition (specifically, glide strengthening to a nasal consonant), Place Assimilation (maintaining palatal place of articulation)

examples.note: Nasalization of /j/ to [ɲ] in the context of nasal harmony, sometimes optional.
Documented as a sound change in the Northern Thai dialect.

sources: O'Hagan, Z. (2013). Syllable structure, nasal harmony and phonemicization in Tupinambá. Course paper, UC Berkeley.
Panyaatisin, K. (2013). Dialect maintenance, shift and variation in a Northern Thai industrial estate. University of Essex.
